Implements a workout dialog interface with text, subtext, and action bar management for Pebble firmware.

# Purpose
The code defines a module for managing a `WorkoutDialog` in a graphical user interface. It provides functionality to create, initialize, and manage the lifecycle of a dialog window that can display text, subtext, and icons, and includes an action bar for user interaction. The module includes functions to load, appear, and unload the dialog window, as well as to set text and subtext content. It also manages the action bar's visibility and configuration, allowing for user input through button interactions.

The module is designed to be integrated into a larger application, as indicated by its use of external libraries and resources, such as `applib_malloc`, `gtypes`, and `resource_ids`. It defines several functions that serve as public APIs for creating and manipulating `WorkoutDialog` instances, such as [`workout_dialog_create`](<#workout_dialog_create>), [`workout_dialog_init`](<#workout_dialog_init>), and [`workout_dialog_set_text`](<#workout_dialog_set_text>). The code also handles memory management for dialog components, ensuring that resources are allocated and freed appropriately. The dialog's appearance and behavior are customizable, with options to set text alignment, font styles, and icon positions based on the display type (rectangular or round).
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `workout_dialog.h`
- `applib/applib_malloc.auto.h`
- `applib/graphics/gtypes.h`
- `applib/ui/dialogs/dialog_private.h`
- `kernel/ui/kernel_ui.h`
- `kernel/pbl_malloc.h`
- `resource/resource_ids.auto.h`
- `system/passert.h`

### prv\_workout\_dialog\_load<!-- {{#callable:prv_workout_dialog_load}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L29>)

Initializes and configures the layout and components of a workout dialog window.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object that the workout dialog will be loaded into.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the `WorkoutDialog` and `Dialog` objects associated with the `window`.
    - Get the root layer of the `window` to determine layout bounds.
    - Create an icon for the dialog and determine its size.
    - Determine whether to show the action bar based on `workout_dialog->hide_action_bar`.
    - Calculate various layout margins and offsets based on the presence of the action bar and icon size.
    - If the status layer is to be shown, add it to the dialog.
    - Calculate text layout attributes, including alignment and spacing, and create text attributes for round displays.
    - Determine the height of the text and adjust margins for single or multi-line text.
    - Initialize and configure the main text layer with calculated parameters and add it to the window's layer hierarchy.
    - If a subtext buffer exists, initialize and configure a subtext layer and add it to the window's layer hierarchy.
    - If the action bar is to be shown, add it to the window.
    - Calculate the position for the icon based on display type (rectangular or round) and add it to the window's layer hierarchy.
    - Call `dialog_load` to finalize the dialog setup.
- **Output**: The function does not return a value; it sets up the dialog's visual components within the provided `window`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)
    - [`GRect`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GRect>)
    - [`graphics_text_attributes_create`](<../../../applib/graphics/text_layout.c.md#graphics_text_attributes_create>)
    - [`text_layer_init_with_parameters`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_init_with_parameters>)
    - [`text_layer_enable_screen_text_flow_and_paging`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_enable_screen_text_flow_and_paging>)
    - [`text_layer_set_line_spacing_delta`](<../../../applib/ui/text_layer.c.md#text_layer_set_line_spacing_delta>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_add_to_window`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_add_to_window>)
    - [`grect_get_max_x`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#grect_get_max_x>)
    - [`GPoint`](<../../../applib/graphics/gtypes.h.md#GPoint>)

### prv\_workout\_dialog\_appear<!-- {{#callable:prv_workout_dialog_appear}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L167>)

Triggers the appearance of a workout dialog by calling the `dialog_appear` function on the dialog associated with the given window.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to a `Window` object that contains user data for the workout dialog.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the `WorkoutDialog` object associated with the `window` using [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>).
    - Call the `dialog_appear` function with the `dialog` member of the `WorkoutDialog` to make the dialog appear.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)

### prv\_workout\_dialog\_unload<!-- {{#callable:prv_workout_dialog_unload}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L172>)

Releases resources and memory associated with a `WorkoutDialog` when a window is unloaded.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``window``: A pointer to the `Window` object from which to unload the `WorkoutDialog`.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Retrieve the `WorkoutDialog` from the `window`'s user data.
    - Call `dialog_unload` to release resources associated with the dialog.
    - Remove and deinitialize the `action_bar` from the window.
    - Deinitialize the `confirm_icon` and `decline_icon` bitmaps.
    - Free the `subtext_buffer` if it exists.
    - Free the `WorkoutDialog` if `destroy_on_pop` is true.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs cleanup operations.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_get_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_get_user_data>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_remove_from_window`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_remove_from_window>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_deinit`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_deinit>)
    - [`gbitmap_deinit`](<../../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_deinit>)

### workout\_dialog\_init<!-- {{#callable:workout_dialog_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L191>)

Initializes a `WorkoutDialog` structure with a dialog and action bar, setting up icons and window handlers.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``workout_dialog``: A pointer to a `WorkoutDialog` structure to initialize.
    - ``dialog_name``: A string representing the name of the dialog.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assert that `workout_dialog` is not null using `PBL_ASSERTN`.
    - Initialize `workout_dialog` to an empty `WorkoutDialog` structure.
    - Call `dialog_init` to initialize the dialog within `workout_dialog` with `dialog_name`.
    - Retrieve the `Window` from the dialog and set its handlers for load, unload, and appear events using [`window_set_window_handlers`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_window_handlers>).
    - Set the user data for the window to `workout_dialog` using [`window_set_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_user_data>).
    - Initialize the `confirm_icon` and `decline_icon` bitmaps with resources `RESOURCE_ID_ACTION_BAR_ICON_CHECK` and `RESOURCE_ID_ACTION_BAR_ICON_X`, respectively.
    - Initialize the `action_bar` within `workout_dialog` using [`action_bar_layer_init`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_init>).
    - Set the icons for the `action_bar` for the up and down buttons using [`action_bar_layer_set_icon`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_icon>).
    - Set the background color of the `action_bar` to black using [`action_bar_layer_set_background_color`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_background_color>).
    - Set the context of the `action_bar` to `workout_dialog` using [`action_bar_layer_set_context`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_context>).
- **Output**: None (void function).
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`window_set_window_handlers`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_window_handlers>)
    - [`window_set_user_data`](<../../../applib/ui/window.c.md#window_set_user_data>)
    - [`gbitmap_init_with_resource`](<../../../applib/graphics/gbitmap.c.md#gbitmap_init_with_resource>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_init`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_init>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_icon`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_icon>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_background_color`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_background_color>)
    - [`action_bar_layer_set_context`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_context>)

### workout\_dialog\_create<!-- {{#callable:workout_dialog_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L215>)

Allocates and initializes a `WorkoutDialog` structure with a given dialog name.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``dialog_name``: A constant character pointer representing the name of the dialog to initialize.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Allocate memory for a `WorkoutDialog` structure using `applib_malloc`.
    - Check if the memory allocation was successful.
    - If successful, call [`workout_dialog_init`](<#workout_dialog_init>) to initialize the `WorkoutDialog` with the provided `dialog_name`.
    - Return the pointer to the initialized `WorkoutDialog` structure.
- **Output**: A pointer to the newly created and initialized `WorkoutDialog` structure, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`workout_dialog_init`](<#workout_dialog_init>)

### workout\_dialog\_set\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable:workout_dialog_set_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L232>)

Sets the click configuration provider for the action bar of a `WorkoutDialog`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``workout_dialog``: A pointer to a `WorkoutDialog` structure.
    - ``click_config_provider``: A function pointer to a `ClickConfigProvider` that defines the click configuration.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `workout_dialog` is `NULL`; if it is, return immediately.
    - Get the `action_bar` from the `workout_dialog`.
    - Call [`action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider`](<../../../applib/ui/action_bar_layer.c.md#action_bar_layer_set_click_config_provider>) with the `action_bar` and `click_config_provider` to set the click configuration provider.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
- **Functions Called**:

### workout\_dialog\_set\_subtext<!-- {{#callable:workout_dialog_set_subtext}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L265>)

Sets the subtext of a `WorkoutDialog` by allocating memory for the new text and copying it into the dialog's subtext buffer.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``workout_dialog``: A pointer to a `WorkoutDialog` structure where the subtext will be set.
    - ``text``: A constant character pointer to the text string to be set as the subtext.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Check if `workout_dialog->subtext_buffer` is not NULL, and if so, free the existing memory using `applib_free`.
    - Calculate the length of the input `text` using `strlen`.
    - Allocate memory for the new subtext using `applib_malloc`, with a size of `len + 1` to accommodate the null terminator.
    - Copy the input `text` into the newly allocated `subtext_buffer` using `strncpy`, including the null terminator.
- **Output**: No return value; the function modifies the `subtext_buffer` of the `WorkoutDialog` structure in place.

### workout\_dialog\_set\_action\_bar\_hidden<!-- {{#callable:workout_dialog_set_action_bar_hidden}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/apps/system_apps/workout/workout_dialog.c#L274>)

Sets the visibility of the action bar in a `WorkoutDialog`.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``workout_dialog``: A pointer to a `WorkoutDialog` structure where the action bar visibility will be set.
    - ``should_hide``: A boolean value indicating whether the action bar should be hidden (`true`) or shown (`false`).
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Assigns the value of `should_hide` to the `hide_action_bar` member of the `workout_dialog` structure.
- **Output**: No return value.
